title: Inducing tumor cell necrosis by calibrated local infusion of noxious fluids
abstract: Infusion therapy for primary or metastatic tumors includes infusion of fluids that are into supplying blood vessels at concentration and flow rates that kill tumor cells or but don't kill normal cells. The infused fluids can be known toxins, or substances generally considered non-toxic that in high enough flow rates nevertheless cause cancer cell death.
claims:
Claims I CLAIM: 1. A method of treating a tumor in an organ of a patient having at least one blood vessel which supplies blood to said organ and said tumor, the method comprising: infusing a fluid comprising at least one substance into the at least one blood vessel at a concentration and rate of said at least one substance sufficient to kill cells of said tumor by direct toxicity, said direct toxicity being mediated through direct disruption of cells, including rupturing cell membranes or denaturing cellular proteins, and not mediated through disruption of biochemical processes of said cells, said concentration and rate being insufficient to kill nontumorous organ cells. 2. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 1, where said fluid infused is not a medication. 3. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 1 , wherein said organ is the liver. 4. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 3, wherein the at least one blood vessel is the hepatic artery, and wherein said infusing does not result in the hepatic artery being occluded. 5. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 3, wherein the at least one blood vessel is the portal vein, and wherein said infusing does not result in the portal vein being occluded. 6. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 3, wherein the at least one blood vessel is the hepatic artery and the portal vein. 7. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 1, wherein said fluid an acidic solution. 8. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 1, wherein said concentration and rate are sufficient so that said infusing results in death of tumor cells, without thrombosis of 

 the vessel infused. 9. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 1, where said at least one toxic substance is selected from the group consisting of hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid, carbonic acid, acetic acid, citric acid, and combinations thereof. 10. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 1, wherein said fluid is a basic solution. 11. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 10, where said at least one toxic substance is selected from the group consisting of sodium hydroxide, sodium bicarbonate, potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, magnesium hydroxide, and combinations thereof. 12. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 1, wherein said fluid is a hypertonic fluid. 13. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 12, wherein said hypertonic fluid is hypertonic relative to the patient's blood. 14. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 12, where the hypertonic fluid is selected from the group consisting of 3% saline solution, hypertonic saline solution, saline solution with osmolarity >300 mg/dl, and combinations thereof. 15. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 1, wherein said fluid is a hypotonic fluid. 16. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 15, wherein said hypotonic fluid is hypotonic relative to the patient's blood. 17. A method of treating tumors as in claim 15, wherein said hypotonic fluid is selected from the group consisting of distilled water, hypotonic saline solution, and combinations thereof. 18. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 1, wherein said at least one toxic substance is an organic solvent. 19. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 18, wherein the organic solvent is an alcohol. 20. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 1, wherein said at least one toxic substance is a poison. 21. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 20, wherein said poison contains cyanide. 22. A method of treating a tumor in an organ of a patient having at least one feeding blood vessel which provided blood to said organ and to said tumor, the method comprising: infusing a fluid having a temperature above 104 degrees Fahrenheit into the at least one blood vessel at a rate sufficient to kill cells of said tumor. 23. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 22, where said fluid is not a medication. 23. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 22, wherein said infusing does not result in the at least one blood vessel being occluded. 25. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 22, wherein said fluid is selected from the group consisting of water, saline solution, contrast fluid, oil, and combinations thereof. 26. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 22, wherein said organ is selected from the group consisting of the liver, the lung, the brain, and the prostate gland. 27. A method of treating a tumor in an organ of a patient having at least one feeding blood vessel supplying blood to said organ and to said tumor, the method comprising: infusing a fluid having a temperature below 85 degrees Fahrenheit into the at least one feeding blood vessel at a rate sufficient to kill cells of said tumor. 28. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 27, where said fluid is not a medication. 29. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 27, wherein said infusing does not result in the at least one blood vessel being occluded. 30. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 27, wherein said fluid is selected from the group consisting of water, saline solution, contrast fluid, and combinations thereof. 31. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 25, wherein said organ is selected from the group consisting of the liver, the lung, the brain, and the prostate gland. 32. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 1, further comprising: calibrating the fluid during said infusing, including measuring a concentration of the toxic substance in the organ's parenchyma or in venous blood from said organ, and changing the concentration of said toxic substance in said fluid based on said measuring. 33. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 22, further comprising: calibrating the fluid during said infusing, including measuring the temperature of the organ's parenchyma or in venous blood from said organ, and changing the temperature of said fluid based on said measuring. 34. A method of treating a tumor according to Claim 27, further comprising: calibrating the fluid during said infusing, including 

 measuring the temperature of the organ's parenchyma or of venous blood from said organ, and changing the temperature of said fluid based on said measuring.
